title: A wave of dissent sweeps through Servant of the People, Zelensky's party
subhead: As Ukraine prepares to pass a series of laws in order to secure international funding, the president appears to have lost some of his influence over his parliamentary majority. His loyal supporters are trying to keep his party cohesive, as it's deeply connected to his rise to power.

title: 'Historic day': Artemis II astronauts travel further into space than ever before
Trump doubles down on Iran threat, says ceasefire 'not good enough'
On immigration, Spain moves against the tide of its European neighbors
subhead: Citing economic and moral arguments, Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's Socialist government is preparing to grant legal status to 500,000 undocumented workers, most from Latin America. The plan brings hope to the 840,000 foreign nationals living without papers in Spain.

title: In Hungary, unease grows over Viktor Orban's crony capitalism
subhead: Companies that have thrived on public contracts since the Fidesz party came to power in 2010 have seen their share prices tumble ahead of the April 12 legislative elections, as polls indicate that opposition candidate Péter Magyar could win.

title: Sex is an art form hidden in the shadows
subhead: Moral panic over the pleasures of the flesh prevents us from recognizing the artistic dimension of sexuality, as well as from passing down the best practices, argues Le Monde columnist  Maïa Mazaurette.

title: Are Trump's ideologues intellectuals or imposters?
subhead: Curtis Yarvin has become a leading figure in the Neo-Reactionary movement (also known as the Dark Enlightenment), which has now made it to the White House. But can this blogger really be considered an intellectual? Political theorist Arnaud Miranda and sociologist Olivier Alexandre debate the origins and roles of one of the thinkers who has Trump's ear.

Will Self: 'Our historical condition is a peculiar mixture of omniscience and impotence'
How 'The Camp of the Saints' became the far right's cult novel, from the Le Pens to MAGA
subhead: Since its publication in 1973, Jean Raspail's novel has shaped the imagination of ideologues who oppose immigration. The openly racist story, which envisions a million Indians descending on the French coast, was recently republished across the Atlantic.

title: China's execution of a Frenchman convicted of drug trafficking casts a chill on relations with Paris
subhead: Chan Thao Phoumy was sentenced in 2010 after authorities dismantled a methamphetamine trafficking network. France has condemned the fact that his defense was not allowed to attend his final court hearing.

title: Le Duc brings the fresh taste of the sea to Paris
subhead: For nearly 60 years, Le Duc, a family-run Parisian institution known for its delicate fish recipes, has drawn a loyal following who come to savor impossibly thinly sliced, pearly raw sea bass or langoustines with red shiso and wasabi.
